title: Revert the unplanned pre-identity-OID escape hatch (skipIdentityStamp / tryReadSubstrateIdForDir); any pre-identity re-sync support must be designed via canonical process
context: Commit 0fcba9c added three artifacts — tryReadSubstrateIdForDir (context-dir.ts), the skipIdentityStamp option on writeBlockForDir (block-api.ts), and its use in resyncSchema (index.ts:574-575) — to work around an identity-stamping throw when /context install --update's migration-aware schema re-sync runs against a substrate with no established substrate_id. It addressed a real migrate-path test failure in TASK-026 (S4 / FGAP-029). The change was made ad-hoc inside the TASK-026 work via an over-scoped sub-agent brief, with no declared gap, no DEC, and no scoped plan — the unplanned-entry pattern canonical process exists to prevent. Whether the change is technically correct is not the question; correctness does not authorize unplanned architectural surface.
decision: Revert all three artifacts: remove tryReadSubstrateIdForDir from context-dir.ts, remove the skipIdentityStamp option and its gate from writeBlockForDir in block-api.ts, and remove the probe and flag usage from resyncSchema in index.ts. Restore mandatory identity-stamping on every write, including schema re-sync. The migrate-path test that the escape hatch satisfied will fail again; restoring it green is a separate, canonically-planned implementation. Any genuine requirement for resync to support a pre-identity substrate will be designed via canonical process — a declared gap, a dedicated DEC, and a scoped plan — not folded into another task's brief.
consequences: The three artifacts are removed; identity stamping is again mandatory on every write, with a loud throw on a pre-identity substrate (guards against mis-provisioning).
TASK-026's migrate-path test fails again, re-surfacing the pre-identity schema-re-sync problem as an explicit, unmasked open problem.
Closing TASK-026 / S4 now requires a canonical decision on how (or whether) pre-identity re-sync is supported, governed by a future DEC and plan.
issue-001 (the migrations.json stray-decl rollback defect) is orthogonal and remains open; unaffected by this reversal.

references.label: the in-progress S4 task this decision governs
the canonically-derived issue this arc surfaced
